biology.title: Biology
biology.overview: Biology covers the science of all living things — from molecules and cells through whole organisms to ecosystems and evolutionary change. UK Biology BSc programmes are typically broad in Years 1–2 (covering cell biology, genetics, evolution, physiology, ecology), then narrow in Year 3 to a specialism (e.g. molecular biology, ecology, animal behaviour, plant science, microbiology). Most courses include compulsory field work; many include an optional placement year or year abroad.

modules: Optional modules (Conservation, Genomics, Plant Sciences, Neuroscience etc.)

professionalBodies.name: Royal Society of Biology (RSB)
professionalBodies.benefit: RSB-accredited programmes lead to Associate Membership (AMRSB) and chartered status (CBiol) with experience

biology.employerPerspective: Biology graduates are the bedrock of the UK life sciences economy (R&D pharma, biotech, conservation NGOs, environmental consultancy). Strong demand from pharma R&D and the growing UK biotech cluster. Conservation careers are competitive but expanding via NERC and Defra. PhD is the usual entry to academic research.
biology.entryRequirements: Typical offer: AAA–AAB at A-level including Biology, plus one of Chemistry, Maths or Physics. GCSE: Maths and English at grade 6/B or above. Some universities prefer Chemistry as the second subject. Strong evidence of independent reading or work experience expected.
biology.furtherStudy: MSc Conservation Biology, MSc Bioinformatics, MSc Molecular Biology. PhD routes via UKRI DTPs are the standard path to research careers.
isItForYou: Study this if you're fascinated by how living things work, from cells to ecosystems
Study this if you enjoy lab work, field work, and the slow patient process of scientific discovery
Think twice if you don't enjoy chemistry — a lot of biology at degree level is biochemistry; or if you want a fast-track to high salaries (life-sciences pay scales slower than software)

zoology.title: Zoology
zoology.overview: Zoology is the study of animal life — anatomy, physiology, evolution, behaviour and conservation across vertebrate and invertebrate taxa. UK Zoology degrees emphasise field-work (often including expeditions to South Africa, Madagascar, Borneo or Costa Rica) and laboratory skills, with Year 3 specialism options ranging from primatology and marine mammals to entomology and behavioural ecology.

modules: Optional modules (Primatology, Marine Mammals, Entomology etc.)

skills: Field identification of UK and tropical species
Animal handling and ethical research design
zoology.employerPerspective: Most zoology graduates enter conservation, ecological consultancy or further study. Field experience and volunteering hours are critical for the conservation route — placements and overseas field courses materially help.
zoology.entryRequirements: Typical offer: AAA–AAB including Biology plus one more science. GCSE: Maths and English at 6/B or above.
zoology.furtherStudy: MSc Conservation Biology, MSc Animal Behaviour, MSc Primate Behaviour. PhD routes for academic careers.
isItForYou: Study this if your passion is animals and you want serious field-work alongside academic study
Study this if you can balance the rewards with the modest salary trajectory of conservation careers
Think twice if you specifically want to be a vet — you'd want Veterinary Medicine, not Zoology

marineBiology.title: Marine Biology
marineBiology.overview: Marine Biology studies life in the ocean — from plankton and coral reefs to whales and deep-sea ecosystems. UK Marine Biology degrees are concentrated at coastal universities (Plymouth, Bangor, St Andrews, Newcastle, Southampton) with on-site research vessels and marine laboratories. Programmes typically include SCUBA training, marine field-courses (often the Caribbean or Mediterranean), and dissertation projects on real marine populations.

modules: Optional modules (Fisheries, Coral Reefs, Marine Mammals)

skills: Boat handling and SCUBA scientific diving

professionalBodies.name: Marine Biological Association (MBA)
professionalBodies.benefit: Student membership; access to Plymouth marine library

professionalBodies.name: Royal Society of Biology
professionalBodies.benefit: AMRSB / CBiol pathway via accredited degrees

marineBiology.employerPerspective: UK marine biology graduates are well placed in the growing marine renewables industry (offshore wind environmental impact assessments), fisheries science (Cefas), and marine conservation NGOs. Aquaculture is expanding rapidly. SCUBA training and overseas field-work significantly improve job prospects.
marineBiology.entryRequirements: Typical offer: AAA–ABB including Biology and ideally Chemistry. GCSE: Maths and English at 6/B or above.
marineBiology.furtherStudy: MSc Marine Biology, MSc Marine Conservation, MSc Oceanography. PhD via NERC DTPs.
isItForYou: Study this if you love being in or near the sea and want a hands-on, field-heavy degree
Study this if you're prepared for cold UK coastline field-work as well as tropical reefs
Think twice if you don't enjoy chemistry or maths — modern marine biology is increasingly quantitative

ecology.title: Ecology & Conservation
ecology.overview: Ecology and Conservation Biology focuses on the relationships between living organisms and their environment, and on applied science to protect biodiversity. The UK has world-leading conservation science, with NERC funding research on climate adaptation, rewilding, invasive species management and ecosystem services. Programmes are field-intensive and include practical conservation work with NGOs, national parks, and rewilding projects.

modules: Optional modules (Rewilding, Climate Change, Tropical Ecology)

skills: Habitat surveying (Phase 1, NVC) and species identification
GIS, spatial modelling and remote sensing

professionalBodies.name: Chartered Institute of Ecology and Environmental Management (CIEEM)
professionalBodies.benefit: Student membership leading to chartered status (CEnv, CEcol) with experience

professionalBodies.name: British Ecological Society
professionalBodies.benefit: Student membership, conferences and small grants

ecology.employerPerspective: Ecological consultancy is the largest UK employer for Ecology graduates — driven by environmental impact assessments for housing, wind farms, HS2 etc. Conservation NGO careers are competitive but expanding; chartered status (CEnv/CEcol) is the recognised credential.
ecology.entryRequirements: Typical offer: AAB–ABB including Biology. GCSE: Maths and English at 6/B or above.
ecology.furtherStudy: MSc Conservation, MSc Ecology and Environmental Sustainability, MSc GIS. PhD routes via NERC DTPs.
isItForYou: Study this if you want to apply science directly to protect biodiversity and reverse environmental decline
Study this if you enjoy mixing field-work with data analysis and policy engagement
Think twice if you want pure lab-based research — this discipline is field- and policy-heavy

plantSciences.title: Plant Sciences
plantSciences.overview: Plant Sciences (Botany) studies the biology of plants — from molecular genetics and biochemistry to ecology and agricultural application. With food security and climate adaptation as central global challenges, UK Plant Science research is heavily funded by UKRI/BBSRC. Specialisms include crop science, plant-pathogen interactions, photosynthesis biophysics, and tropical/temperate plant ecology.

modules: Optional modules (Plant Pathology, Agroecology, Plant Biotech)

plantSciences.employerPerspective: UK plant biotech and crop science are world-leading. Employers value molecular biology + bioinformatics skills. Climate adaptation and sustainable agriculture funding from BBSRC and UKRI is expanding rapidly.
plantSciences.entryRequirements: Typical offer: AAA–ABB including Biology, ideally Chemistry too. GCSE: Maths and English at 6/B or above.
plantSciences.furtherStudy: MSc Plant Sciences, MSc Crop Science, MSc Agroecology. PhD via UKRI DTPs.
isItForYou: Study this if you're interested in solving food security, sustainability or climate adaptation through plants
Study this if you enjoy molecular biology — modern plant sciences is highly molecular
Think twice if you primarily want to work with animals — pick zoology instead

biotechnology.title: Biotechnology
biotechnology.overview: Biotechnology applies biological systems and organisms to develop products and processes — from vaccines and therapeutics to industrial enzymes and biofuels. The UK has a thriving biotech sector concentrated around Cambridge, Oxford, Manchester and Edinburgh. Programmes combine deep biological sciences (genetics, microbiology, biochemistry) with applied engineering and entrepreneurship.

modules: Optional modules (Pharma Biotech, Industrial Biotech, Agri-biotech)

professionalBodies.name: BioIndustry Association (BIA)
professionalBodies.benefit: UK biotech employer network; student events

biotechnology.employerPerspective: UK biotech is one of Europe's largest sectors, with a strong Cambridge cluster (e.g. AstraZeneca HQ) and growing Manchester / Edinburgh clusters. Demand for biotech-trained R&D scientists and bioprocess engineers consistently exceeds supply.
biotechnology.entryRequirements: Typical offer: AAA–AAB including Biology and Chemistry. GCSE: Maths and English at 6/B or above.
biotechnology.furtherStudy: MSc Biotechnology, MSc Bioinformatics, MSc Pharmaceutical Sciences. PhD routes via UKRI DTPs.
isItForYou: Study this if you love biology AND want to apply it to industry, healthcare or sustainability
Study this if you want a clear route into the UK biopharma sector
Think twice if you want pure academic biology — biotech is applied science

earthSciences.title: Earth Sciences
earthSciences.overview: Earth Sciences is the broad study of our planet — its structure, history, processes and inhabitants — combining geology, geophysics, geochemistry, oceanography, climate science and planetary science. Modern Earth Sciences degrees integrate field-work, lab-based analysis and computational modelling. Major UK programmes (Oxford, Cambridge, Imperial, UCL, Edinburgh) feed into both academic research and the resource extraction industry.
skills: Mineral and rock identification (hand specimen and microscope)

professionalBodies.name: Geological Society of London (CGeol)
professionalBodies.benefit: Accredited degrees + 4 years experience → Chartered Geologist (CGeol)

earthSciences.employerPerspective: Traditional oil & gas employer demand has fallen, but mining (critical minerals for batteries/renewables), geotechnical consultancy (HS2, offshore wind), and environmental/hydrogeology consultancy are growing. CGeol is the recognised credential.
earthSciences.entryRequirements: Typical offer: A*AA–AAA including Maths plus one of Geology, Physics, Chemistry, Biology. GCSE: Maths and English at 6/B or above.
earthSciences.furtherStudy: MSc Petroleum Geoscience, MSc Engineering Geology, MSc Geophysics. PhD via NERC DTPs.
isItForYou: Study this if you want a broad geosciences foundation and aren't sure whether you'll specialise in geology, geophysics, climate or paleontology
Study this if you enjoy fieldwork in remote locations (UK Lake District, Iceland, Spain)
Think twice if you specifically know you want resource geology — pick Geology directly

geology.title: Geology
geology.overview: Geology is the study of the solid Earth — rocks, minerals, structural processes, surface processes and Earth history. UK Geology BSc programmes are field-intensive and lead to Chartered Geologist (CGeol) status with experience. Field areas include the Scottish Highlands, Lake District, Spain (Cabo de Gata) and Iceland. The Independent Mapping Project (an 8-week solo geological survey) is the defining experience of UK Geology degrees.
skills: Rock and mineral identification (hand specimen + thin section)
Drill core logging and geological logging software

professionalBodies.name: Geological Society of London (CGeol)
professionalBodies.benefit: Accredited geology degrees provide the academic route to Chartered Geologist (CGeol) status with 4–6 years experience

geology.employerPerspective: Resource geology (mining for critical minerals — lithium, cobalt, copper for the green transition) and geotechnical consultancy (HS2, offshore wind anchoring) are the strongest current markets. Oil and gas is contracting but still pays a premium. CGeol is the gold-standard credential.
geology.entryRequirements: Typical offer: A*AA–AAB including Maths plus Geology or another science. GCSE: Maths and English at 6/B or above.
geology.furtherStudy: MSc Petroleum Geoscience, MSc Engineering Geology, MSc Mineral Exploration. PhD via NERC DTPs.
isItForYou: Study this if you love being outdoors and want a discipline where field-work is central to the science
Study this if you're excited by the energy transition and critical minerals supply chain
Think twice if you don't enjoy physics or chemistry — modern geology is highly quantitative

environmentalScience.title: Environmental Science
environmentalScience.overview: Environmental Science is the interdisciplinary study of how natural systems work and how human activity is changing them — climate change, pollution, biodiversity loss, water resources, sustainability policy. UK Environmental Science programmes integrate atmospheric science, ecology, geochemistry, geography and policy, with strong practical field-work and consultancy-aligned skills.

modules: Optional modules (Climate Change, Marine Pollution, Renewable Energy)

professionalBodies.name: Society for the Environment (CEnv)
professionalBodies.benefit: Chartered Environmentalist status with 4 years experience

environmentalScience.employerPerspective: Environmental consultancy is the largest UK graduate employer — driven by EIA requirements for HS2, offshore wind, housing. Corporate sustainability (ESG roles) is the fastest-growing graduate market. Chartered status (CEnv) is the recognised credential.
environmentalScience.entryRequirements: Typical offer: AAB–ABB including at least one science. GCSE: Maths and English at 6/B or above.
environmentalScience.furtherStudy: MSc Environmental Management, MSc Climate Change, MSc Sustainability. PhD via NERC DTPs.
isItForYou: Study this if you want to combine science with policy and practical impact on sustainability
Study this if you're drawn to the rapidly-expanding environmental consultancy/ESG industry
Think twice if you want pure laboratory science — Env Sci is interdisciplinary and field/policy-focused

geography.title: Geography
geography.duration: 3 years (BA or BSc) / 4 years (MGeog or with placement)
geography.overview: Geography is the study of places, environments and the relationships between people and the planet. UK Geography degrees offer a BA (human geography emphasis — urbanism, development, geopolitics) or BSc (physical geography emphasis — glaciology, biogeography, geomorphology); most allow specialisation in Year 2 or 3. Field-work is central, with overseas trips often included (Iceland, Morocco, Berlin, Vietnam).
skills: Policy analysis and writing for non-specialist audiences

professionalBodies.name: Royal Geographical Society (with IBG)
professionalBodies.benefit: Chartered Geographer (CGeog) status with experience; UK's leading geographical society

geography.employerPerspective: Geography graduates are uniquely versatile — equally welcomed by environmental consultancy, urban planning, civil service, GIS firms, and management consultancy. GIS skills are increasingly required across the board. The combination of qualitative and quantitative skills is rare and valued.
geography.entryRequirements: Typical offer: A*AA–AAB. Geography preferred but not always required. GCSE: Maths and English at 6/B or above.
geography.furtherStudy: MSc Urban Planning, MSc Environmental Management, MSc GIS. PhD via UKRI DTPs.
isItForYou: Study this if you're interested in how the world works — places, people, environment, all interconnected
Study this if you want a broad and flexible degree that opens many doors
Think twice if you want a narrow specialist degree — Geography is deliberately broad

forestry.title: Forestry & Countryside Management
forestry.overview: Forestry and Countryside Management trains professionals who manage woodlands, rural landscapes and protected areas. The UK has a small number of specialist programmes (Bangor, Aberdeen, Cumbria, Harper Adams) reflecting niche but stable demand. Net-zero policy and the expansion of UK woodland cover is driving renewed demand for forestry graduates.

modules: Optional modules (Tropical Forestry, Urban Forestry, Forest Economics)

skills: GIS and remote sensing for forest monitoring

professionalBodies.name: Institute of Chartered Foresters (ICF)
professionalBodies.benefit: Professional Member (MICFor) and Chartered status with experience

professionalBodies.name: Royal Forestry Society (RFS)
professionalBodies.benefit: UK's leading forestry charity; CPD and grants

forestry.employerPerspective: Forestry careers are stable and growing — the UK target to plant 30,000 hectares of new woodland per year is driving recruitment at Forestry England/Scotland. Chartered status (MICFor) is the recognised credential.
forestry.entryRequirements: Typical offer: ABB–BBB ideally including Biology or Geography. GCSE: Maths and English at 6/B or above.
forestry.furtherStudy: MSc Forestry, MSc Conservation, MSc Land Management. PhD routes for research careers.
isItForYou: Study this if you want an outdoor career managing woodlands or rural land
Study this if you're excited by the net-zero woodland expansion programme
Think twice if you want a high-paying corporate career — forestry is meaningful but modestly paid
